title: Measurement
objective: Students will explore measurement concepts including Measuring length and height (age 5+), Capacity and volume, Measuring mass and weight and more.

steps.content: Have you ever thought about Measuring length and height (age 5+)? Let's find out together!
Measure and begin to record lengths and heights using non-standard and standard units
Time to try this out: Measure the length of a table using cubes and record the result
If you measures the length of a book using small cubes laid end to end, can they count how many cubes it takes — and then try measuring the same book with a ruler?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Measuring length and height (age 5+). Measure the length of a table using cubes and record the result Begin to use a ruler to measure in centimetres
Have you ever thought about Capacity and volume? Let's find out together!
Measure and begin to record capacity and volume using non-standard and standard units
Time to try this out: Measure capacity by counting how many cups fill a container
If you fills a jug using a small cup, can they count how many cups it takes — and understand that this tells you how much the jug holds?
You did it! Here's what we explored about Capacity and volume: Measure capacity by counting how many cups fill a container Begin to use litres as a unit of capacity
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Measuring mass and weight!
Measure and begin to record mass/weight using non-standard and standard units
Let me show you something cool. Use a balance to compare masses of two objects
If you uses balance scales to compare two objects, can they tell you which is heavier — and if given weights like 100g, can they balance an object on the scale?
Great job! Today we learned about Measuring mass and weight. Use a balance to compare masses of two objects Measure mass using non-standard units (e.g. 'this book weighs 5 cubes')
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Days, Weeks, Months & Years!
Recognise and use language relating to dates, including days of the week, weeks, months, and years
Let me show you something cool. Name the seven days of the week in order
If you ask you what day it is today, what day yesterday was, and what month we're in, can they answer all three — and tell you how many months are in a year?
Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Days, Weeks, Months & Years. Name the seven days of the week in order Name the twelve months of the year in order
Let's discover the amazing world of Telling time to the minute!
Measure and begin to record time in hours, minutes, and seconds
Time to try this out: Use a sand timer or stopwatch to time an activity
If you watches the second hand go all the way round a clock, can they count that as one minute — and estimate roughly how many minutes their favourite TV show lasts?
Great job! Today we learned about Telling time to the minute. Use a sand timer or stopwatch to time an activity Begin to understand that 1 minute = 60 seconds

lessons.title: Telling Time: Hours and Half Hours

steps.content: Welcome to an exciting lesson about Telling Time: Hours and Half Hours!
Tell the time to the hour and half past the hour, and draw clock hands to show these times
Let's practice together! Read an analogue clock showing 3 o'clock
If you point to a clock showing 3 o'clock or half past two, can you tell you the time — and then draw hands on a blank clock face to show a time you call out?
Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Telling Time: Hours and Half Hours. Read an analogue clock showing 3 o'clock Read an analogue clock showing half past 7
Today we're going to learn about Coin Values. Get ready for an adventure!
Recognise and know the value of different coins and notes
Time to try this out: Identify 1p, 2p, 5p, 10p, 20p, 50p, £1 and £2 coins
If you is given a handful of coins, can they name each one — like 2p, 5p, 20p — and sort them from least to most valuable?
Great job! Today we learned about Coin Values. Identify 1p, 2p, 5p, 10p, 20p, 50p, £1 and £2 coins Know that a £2 coin is worth more than a 50p coin
